skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance with iterative eval loops.
skills.author: lordqyxz
skills.trigger: Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

skills.name: python-code
skills.description: Write, refactor, and debug Python code following Pythonic best practices from PEP 8, Effective Python, and Fluent Python. Merged with effective-python for comprehensive code-level guidance.
skills.author: lordqyxz
skills.trigger: Use this skill whenever the user writes, reviews, refactors, or debugs Python code, asks about Python patterns, idioms, or style, mentions writing a script/module/package in Python, or requests code that is Pythonic. Also the implementation target for python-developer skill delegation. Applies to all Python 3.10+ code.

skills.name: vue3-sfc
skills.description: Vue 3 SFC development, modification, and review skill. Write and review .vue files following Vue 3 Composition API + script setup best practices.
skills.author: lordqyxz
skills.trigger: Use when creating, modifying, reviewing, or refactoring .vue files, or any Vue 3 component development task.

skills.name: python-developer
skills.description: Structured Python development workflow: from requirements to delivery using DDD and Clean Architecture. Delegates code implementation to python-code skill.
skills.author: lordqyxz
skills.trigger: Use when starting new feature development, implementing requirements with DDD/Clean Architecture, requirement analysis, domain modeling, or structured architecture-level refactoring.

skills.name: skill-evaluator
skills.description: Evaluate and benchmark Claude Code skills. Run structural validation, trigger accuracy tests, quality evals with automated grading, regression tracking, and generate reports.
skills.author: lordqyxz
skills.trigger: Use when users want to test a skill, benchmark skill performance, compare two skill versions, check skill health, validate skill structure, or run CI-style evals.
